---
title: "group-4-final-report"
author: "Sriharsha Challangi, Kelvin Mai, Thanh Trung Do, Leah Padilla, Tien Hoang"
format: docx
editor: visual
---

# Final Report MATH 4322

## Group 4

Sriharsha Challangi, Kelvin Mai, Thanh Trung Do, Leah Padilla, Tien Hoang

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Introduction

## The dataset contains:

-   10,000 observations
-   19 variables Each observation represents an individual student.

## Description of Variables:

### Response Variable

-   Dropout: Binary variable indicating whether a student drops out. 0 =retained 1 =dropped out

### Predictor Variables

The dataset includes demographic, academic, and socioeconomic predictors:

-   Age: Student age in years
-   Gender: Male / Female
-   Family_Income: Monthly family income
-   Internet_Access: Whether the student has internet access (Yes/No)
-   Study_Hours_per_Day: Average daily study hours
-   Attendance_Rate: Percentage of classes attended
-   Assignment_Delay_Days: Average delay in assignment submission (days)
-   Travel_Time_Minutes: Daily commuting time (minutes)
-   Part_Time_Job: Whether the student works part-time (Yes/No)
-   Scholarship: Scholarship status (Yes/No)
-   Stress_Index: Self-reported stress level on a scale from 1 to 10
-   GPA, Semester_GPA, CGPA: Measures of academic performance
-   Semester: Current year of study (1–4)
-   Department: Academic department (Science, Arts, Business, CS, Engineering)
-   Parental_Education: Highest education level of parents These predictors represent factors that may influence academic performance and the likelihood of student dropout.

## Research Question:

The primary goal of this project is to predict whether a student will drop out based on academic, demographic, and socioeconomic characteristics. More specifically, we aim to address the following research question:

-   Can student demographic and academic factors accurately predict the probability of student dropout?

Our main focus is prediction, but we also want to identify which variables are most strongly associated with dropout risk.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 2. Methods

## Logistic Regression (Leah Padilla, Thanh Trung Do)

## Decision Tree (Sriharsha Challangi, Kelvin Mai, Tien Hoang)

# 3. Results

# 4. Conclusion

# 5. Bibliography
